title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
prompt: Design a class that finds the kth largest element in a stream. Implement `KthLargest(k, nums)` initializer and `add(val) -> number` which returns the kth largest after adding val. Inputs are op protocols of the form `[['KthLargest', k, nums], ['add', v], ...]`; outputs include `null` for the constructor and the kth-largest after each add.
constraints: There will be at least k elements in the heap when add returns.
hints: Keep a min-heap of size k. The top is always the kth largest.
On add: push if size<k; else if val>top, pop and push.
Initialization: feed nums through the same process.
pitfalls: Adding to a sorted array is O(n) per op, too slow at scale.
